question.text: According to SEBI guidelines, expiries of all equity derivatives contracts of an exchange will be uniformly limited to which days?

answer.text: Any working day chosen by the exchange
Last business day of the month only

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI has specified that expiries of all equity derivatives contracts of an exchange will be uniformly limited to either Tuesday or Thursday. Every exchange is allowed one weekly benchmark index options contract on their chosen day. **The Trap:** All other equity derivatives (benchmark index futures, non-benchmark index F&O, single stock F&O) must have minimum 1-month tenor with expiry in the last week of the month on the chosen day. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers equity derivative contract specifications including expiry cycles in detail. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Post-SEBI reforms: weekly options limited to one benchmark index per exchange. All other derivatives = monthly contracts expiring last Tuesday/Thursday. Exchanges need SEBI approval to change settlement day.
